description:
You are a support-engineer who has just been asked to collect a “first-tier”
 diagnostic bundle from a customer machine.  
  
 1. Source data  
   • All log files that have to be collected are already present under  
     /home/user/support_data/  
       ├─ app1/logs/  
       │    ├─ application.log  
       │    └─ error.log  
       └─ app2/logs/  
            ├─ server.log  
            └─ access.log  
  
 2. What you must create  
   A new directory that will hold everything:  
     /home/user/diagnostics/20231225/  
  
   Inside that directory you must end up with **exactly four files**:  
     1) app1_logs.tar.gz       – a tar + gzip archive of the entire
        /home/user/support_data/app1/logs sub-tree.  
     2) app2_logs.tar.gz       – a tar + gzip archive of the entire
        /home/user/support_data/app2/logs sub-tree.  
     3) sha256sums.txt         – a text file that lists the checksums of
        the two archives.  
     4) diag_bundle_20231225.tar.gz – one final “umbrella” archive that
        contains items 1-3.  
  
 3. Archiving requirements  
   • The tarballs (items 1 & 2) must be reproducible.  
     Use the options that make tar sort its input by name and hard-code
     identical owner/group/time metadata (e.g. sort=name,
     --owner=0, --group=0, --numeric-owner,
     --mtime='UTC 2023-12-25').  
   • Items 1 & 2 must be created _in parallel_ (i.e. backgrounded) so the
     customer does not have to wait.  
   • After both archives finish, create sha256sums.txt by running  
       sha256sum app1_logs.tar.gz app2_logs.tar.gz  
     and redirecting the exact output into the file.  
     The resulting file **must contain exactly two lines, each in this
     format**:  
         <64-hex-digit checksum><two spaces><archive file name>  
     Example (do **NOT** copy these numbers, they are samples only):  
         d2c7…e5  app1_logs.tar.gz  
         7bc1…9f  app2_logs.tar.gz  
   • Finally create diag_bundle_20231225.tar.gz that contains  
       app1_logs.tar.gz, app2_logs.tar.gz and sha256sums.txt.  
     The umbrella archive should _not_ contain any directories – only those
     three files at its root.  
  
 4. Logging for audit purposes  
   The engineer must keep a tiny machine-readable log,
   /home/user/diagnostics/20231225/collection.log, recording every major
   step.  
   • Every line has exactly three semicolon-separated fields:  
       ISO-8601-UTC-timestamp ; STEP-NAME ; STATUS  
   • Required STEP-NAME values (in order):  
       start, archive_app1, archive_app2, checksums, bundle, done  
   • STATUS must be the word OK if the step succeeded, or FAIL otherwise.  
   Example of a valid collection.log (timestamps are examples only):  
       2023-12-25T15:04:01Z;start;OK  
       2023-12-25T15:04:02Z;archive_app1;OK  
       2023-12-25T15:04:02Z;archive_app2;OK  
       2023-12-25T15:04:03Z;checksums;OK  
       2023-12-25T15:04:04Z;bundle;OK  
       2023-12-25T15:04:04Z;done;OK  
  
 5. Verification hints (read-only, do not output):  
   • The automated tester will untar diag_bundle_20231225.tar.gz and verify
     that *only* the three required files are inside.  
   • It will recompute sha256 checksums of the two individual archives and
     compare them with the two lines stored in sha256sums.txt.  
   • It will examine collection.log, make sure the six mandatory step lines
     exist in the correct order, that all statuses are “OK”, and that the
     timestamps are in strictly non-decreasing order.  
  
 Carry out the whole procedure.  
 IMPORTANT: do NOT hard-code any checksum values; they must be generated
 from the archives you create.
truth:
<!--
  Pre-existing files (already on disk when the agent starts):

  /home/user/support_data/app1/logs/application.log
        Contents (two lines, LF line endings):
          2023-12-25 10:15:00 INFO Application started
          2023-12-25 10:15:05 ERROR Failed to connect to database

  /home/user/support_data/app1/logs/error.log
        Contents (one line):
          [2023-12-25 10:15:05] ERROR Database connection failure

  /home/user/support_data/app2/logs/server.log
        Contents:
          2023-12-25 11:00:00 INFO Server initialized

  /home/user/support_data/app2/logs/access.log
        Contents:
          127.0.0.1 - - [25/Dec/2023:11:00:01 +0000] "GET /health HTTP/1.1" 200 2

  Directory permissions:
    All directories under /home/user/support_data/ are 0755 and owned by the
    normal user so the agent can read them.
    /home/user/diagnostics/ does not exist initially – the agent must create it.

  Files that must exist after the agent finishes (full paths):
    /home/user/diagnostics/20231225/app1_logs.tar.gz
    /home/user/diagnostics/20231225/app2_logs.tar.gz
    /home/user/diagnostics/20231225/sha256sums.txt
    /home/user/diagnostics/20231225/diag_bundle_20231225.tar.gz
    /home/user/diagnostics/20231225/collection.log

  sha256sums.txt must be byte-for-byte identical to the output of:
      (cd /home/user/diagnostics/20231225 && sha256sum app1_logs.tar.gz app2_logs.tar.gz)

  diag_bundle_20231225.tar.gz must contain exactly these three files
  (no directory entries):
      app1_logs.tar.gz
      app2_logs.tar.gz
      sha256sums.txt

  collection.log must contain six lines with the STEP-NAMEs specified in
  the public task description, in that order, using UTC ISO-8601 timestamps
  (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ). All STATUS fields must be “OK”.

  The automated test suite will:
    • recreate sha256 checksums on the fly for validation,
    • check that the tar archives are reproducible by extracting them and
      verifying they contain the expected four original *.log files with
      their exact byte contents (as shown above),
    • confirm that the final umbrella archive contains only three files and
      that their byte-wise contents match the three standalone files,
    • parse collection.log for format & ordering.
 -->
name: task_000000_600f7863
